APPELLANT'S REQUEST FOR CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED, AND CERTI- FY THAT THE TRANSFER OF THIS CASE TO THE COURT OF APPEAL APPEARS NECESSARY TO SETTLE AN IMPORTANT QUESTION OF LAW, THAT IS, WHO IS THE "PREVAILIN PARTY" SEE FILE FOR DETAILS

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT 01, MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES, CALENDAR OF MAR-29-1996, DENIED EACH OF THE TWO STATUTORY PROVISIONS APPLY TO THIS CONTRACT. BOTH ESTABLISH A RIGHT TO ATTORNEY'S FEES, BUT SECTION 1717(B)(2) CCP DEFINES WHO IS THE PREVAILING PARTY: "WHERE AN ACTION HAS BEEN VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED THERE SHALL BE NO PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF THIS SECTION."

REMITTITUR FILED SUPERIOR COURT FINDS THAT NEITHER C.C. 1717 NOR C.C.P 1032 DIRECTS THE DEFINITION OF WHO IS THE PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF C.C. 2983.4 OF THE REES-LEVERING ACT. THAT DETERMINATION MUST BE MADE FOLLOWING AN ANALYSIS OF THE RELEVANT FACTS AT THE TRIAL LEVEL. ACCORDINGLY, THE TRIAL COURT'S ORDER IN THIS CASE IS VACATED AND THE MATTER REMAN- DED FOR FURTHER PROCEEDINGS CONSISTENT WITH THIS OPINION.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT RECOVER FROM PLAINTIFF DAMIAN, L. THE COURT HEREBY ORDERS THAT DEFENDANT ROMANA TAMONDONG HAVE AND RECOVER AGAINST PLAINTIFF L. DAMIAN REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES IN THE SUM OF $74,710.00 PLUS COSTS OF $1,100.00 FOR A TOTAL AWARD OF $75,810.00, SAID SUM TO BE PAID DIRECTLY TO ATTORNEY ANDREW J. OGILVIE.
